description: Golden identifier set the automated pipeline should retrieve/resolve for the chest-radiograph pneumonia triage / CADe reference case (Case 8). Companion to the HRV/DR/warfarin/sepsis/AFib/melanoma/depression goldens. This is a DEVICE / Software-as-a-Medical-Device imaging case (intervention_type=device) and is regulatory-POSITIVE at the CLASS level with a pneumonia-SPECIFICITY null twist: the operative U.S. pathway EXISTS and is directly retrievable - Class II 510(k) under the radiological computer-assisted software framework, product code QFM (Radiological Computer-Assisted Prioritization Software For Lesions, 21 CFR 892.2080) for the triage/CADt claim, with QAS (notification variant, same 892.2080) and the 892.2090 CADe/CADx detection family (QDQ lesions-suspicious-for-cancer, QBS fracture) as the adjacent codes - AND there is a real predicate pool of cleared CXR-triage 510(k)s under QFM (Lunit/VUNO/Annalise/Milvue/Zebra/RadLogics). The NULL is narrower than HRV/warfarin/depression: NO cleared 510(k) and NO product code is SPECIFIC TO PNEUMONIA CXR triage/CADe - the cleared QFM devices target pneumothorax, effusion, trauma, or general CXR triage, so a pneumonia-CADe claim has no exact predicate code and may require De Novo or predication-by-analogy (to be confirmed with FDA). openFDA has no device/denovo endpoint (404), so the founding De Novo grants for QFM/QAS/QDQ/QBS could not be verified this session and are NOT cited. Case 8 is the slate's 2nd BROAD-PARENT condition (pneumonia) WITH retrievable golden literature, so it is the decisive test of the '+hierarchy' MeSH axis - BUT it will re-hit Case-4 Finding B (the bare condition token 'pneumonia' is crowded off the 5 model-anchored MeSH candidate slots before lookup) until the deferred engine-hardening fix lands, so '+hierarchy' is expected to still no-op; this is documented honestly in the sweep, not asserted-away. Use in the ablation harness: score recall = golden ids present in the bundle's records (FDA codes / K EXACT; literature as a >=K resolvable-ID floor per COMPARISON.md Lesson 2), precision = on-topic rate. Every id in 'required' resolved live 2026-07-09 (26/26 PMIDs via E-utils esummary, 26/26 DOIs via Crossref works, 4/4 product codes via openFDA device/classification, 6/6 K-numbers via openFDA device/510k).
reference_case: Chest-radiograph pneumonia triage / CADe: software analyzing frontal chest radiographs (DICOM) to detect findings suspicious for pneumonia and either prioritize the study on the radiologist worklist (computer-assisted triage, CADt) or flag the finding for reader attention (computer-assisted detection, CADe). Intended-use claim = shorten time-to-review and/or assist detection, NOT autonomous diagnosis. Reference standard = expert radiologist adjudication (>=2-3 readers, pre-specified tie-break) +/- follow-up imaging / clinical-microbiologic confirmation. Setting = high-volume hospital radiology (ED + inpatient); population = adults presenting for chest imaging. The pivotal endpoint is claim-matched: time-to-notification for the triage claim, MRMC reader sensitivity/specificity for the detection claim - standalone AUC alone does NOT substitute for the workflow/detection claim.
mesh_note: Condition authored condition-forward ('chest-radiograph pneumonia triage / detection'), so Case-4 Finding A (mechanism-first phrasing burying the condition past the keyword window) cannot bite. 'Pneumonia' maps to MeSH 'Pneumonia' (D011014), a BROAD PARENT under Respiratory Tract Infections / Lung Diseases with narrower children (bacterial/viral/aspiration/pneumococcal etc.), which is EXACTLY why pneumonia was reserved as the decisive '+hierarchy' test. HOWEVER: Case-4 Finding B is expected to bite here - _mesh_candidates(max_candidates=5) fills all 5 slots with model-anchored bigrams (e.g. 'chest radiograph', 'radiograph pneumonia', 'pneumonia triage', 'pneumonia detection', ...) and truncates the BARE token 'pneumonia' before the NCBI MeSH lookup, so the canonical descriptor never resolves and '+hierarchy' has no parent to expand -> it is expected to no-op == baseline. Whether that prediction holds is written up empirically in ablation_findings.md from the sweep, NOT asserted here. The literature here is a curated imaging-methodology + reporting-standard set (CLAIM / STARD-AI / MRMC / CADe-CADx-framework / dataset-shift + a handful of pneumonia-detection-accuracy papers), the same targeting-ceiling profile risk as DR/melanoma - a generic 'chest radiograph pneumonia' query is not guaranteed to rank these into the top-15; the sweep measures actual recall.

match_policy.required_min_coverage: All ids in 'required' should be present for full recall. 'field_grounding' is the per-field breakdown following each source's role in the spec's SOURCE INVENTORY 'Grounded field(s)' column. Pneumonia has NO scored 'ncts': the 5 ClinicalTrials.gov records the spec cites (NCT05117320 / NCT06456203 / NCT06075836 / NCT05963945 / NCT05594485) were used for design/enrollment CONTEXT ONLY - explicitly 'not as performance-benchmark sources and not as reference-standard authorities' - so they are recorded in the non-scored 'context_only_identifiers' block, not in 'required'. Pneumonia has NO 'fda_den_numbers' (openFDA device/denovo 404 this session; founding De Novo grants unverifiable, none cited), NO 'fda_pma_numbers' (software/SaMD, no Class III PMA), and NO 'fda_nda_numbers' (imaging device, not a drug/biologic).
match_policy.resolution_pass: PMID -> E-utils esummary; DOI -> Crossref works; product_code -> openFDA device/classification; K (510k) -> openFDA device/510k k_number. A required id that fails to resolve is a TEST FAILURE, not a silent drop. All 62 required ids re-resolved live 2026-07-09.
match_policy.regulatory_positive_with_pneumonia_speci: POSITIVE at class level: QFM (892.2080, Class II) is the directly-retrieved operative code for the CADt/triage claim and its classification text limits the device to prioritization/notification ('does not provide ... information from the image analysis other than triage and notification') - matching the not-autonomous-diagnosis claim exactly; QAS is the notification-only variant (same regulation); QDQ/QBS are the 892.2090 CADe/CADx detection-family neighbors. A real predicate pool of QFM CXR-triage 510(k)s is cleared (K211733 Lunit, K241439 VUNO, K222179 Annalise, K232410 SmartChest/Milvue, K192320 HealthCXR/Zebra, K193300 AIMI-Triage/RadLogics). NULL twist: NONE of these cleared devices - and no retrieved product code - is SPECIFIC TO PNEUMONIA; they target pneumothorax/effusion/trauma/general-CXR-triage. So a pneumonia-CADe claim has no exact predicate code and may need De Novo or predication-by-analogy at FDA's discretion. De Novo grants: openFDA device/denovo returns 404, so the founding DEN- numbers for QFM/QAS/QDQ/QBS were NOT verifiable and are NOT cited (a coverage gap, not an omission).

context_only_identifiers.description: Identifiers cited in the pneumonia spec for context but deliberately NOT scored. The harness reads only the 'required' block (ablation_harness.py:256), so nothing here is counted as a hit or a miss. Recorded for a complete audit trail.

ncts.reason: AI to Improve Chest X-ray Reading in Acute Dyspnoeic Patients - RCT (INTERVENTIONAL), enrollment 33. Cited to illustrate a randomized reader-effect design for Fields 1/5. The spec states the CT.gov records were used 'for design/enrollment context only - not as performance-benchmark sources and not as reference-standard authorities', so scoring it would penalize correct pipeline behavior.

AI for Chest Radiography: Impact on Economics, Patient Outcomes and Radiology Service Delivery - INTERVENTIONAL, enrollment 10,000. Large prospective effectiveness design exemplar (Fields 1/5), context-only.

Utility of an AI-based CXR Interpretation Tool in Assisting Diagnostic Accuracy, Speed, and Confidence - OBSERVATIONAL, enrollment 33. Reader-assist/speed endpoint exemplar (Fields 1/5), context-only.
Multi-Reader Retrospective Study, Carebot AI CXR - OBSERVATIONAL, enrollment 956. Multi-reader retrospective design/case-set-scale exemplar (Fields 1/5), context-only.
CXR Abnormality Detection Using AI (Carebot), retrospective - OBSERVATIONAL, enrollment 127. Design/enrollment-scale exemplar (Fields 1/5), context-only.

expected_conclusions.regulatory_class: US Class II, 510(k) software device under the radiological computer-assisted software framework. Triage (CADt) claim -> product code QFM, 21 CFR 892.2080; notification-only variant -> QAS (same regulation). Detection (CADe) claim -> the 892.2090 CADe/CADx family (Class II), with QDQ/QBS as retrieved examples in that regulation.
expected_conclusions.operative_pathway_is_positive: Unlike HRV/warfarin/depression (regulatory NULL), the CLASS-level pathway here is POSITIVE and directly retrievable: QFM/892.2080 with a real cleared-predicate pool (K211733/K241439/K222179/K232410/K192320/K193300). The null is confined to pneumonia-SPECIFICITY - no cleared device or code names pneumonia as its target finding.
expected_conclusions.de_novo_unverifiable: openFDA device/denovo returns 404; the founding De Novo grants that created QFM/QAS/QDQ/QBS could not be verified this session and are deliberately NOT cited (coverage gap, not an omission).

regulatory_null_evidence.claim: No FDA product code and no cleared 510(k) is SPECIFIC TO PNEUMONIA chest-radiograph triage/CADe. The class-level pathway EXISTS (QFM/892.2080 for CADt; 892.2090 QDQ/QBS for CADe) with a real predicate pool, but every cleared QFM CXR-triage device targets pneumothorax, pleural effusion, trauma, or general CXR triage - not pneumonia. A pneumonia-CADe claim therefore has no exact predicate code and may require De Novo classification or predication-by-analogy, to be confirmed with FDA.
regulatory_null_evidence.how_verified: openFDA device/classification returned QFM/QAS (892.2080) and QDQ/QBS (892.2090) as the operative Class II codes; openFDA device/510k returned the QFM predicate pool (K211733 Lunit INSIGHT CXR Triage, K241439 VUNO Med-Chest X-ray Triage, K222179 Annalise Enterprise CXR Triage Trauma, K232410 SmartChest/Milvue, K192320 HealthCXR/Zebra, K193300 AIMI-Triage CXR PTX/RadLogics) - all resolve to the named device, applicant, and decision date, and none names pneumonia as its target finding. openFDA has no device/denovo endpoint (404), so De Novo founding grants for these codes are unverifiable via the API and are not cited. 510(k) free-text summary PDFs (which would confirm each device's specific target findings and reported operating points) are not reachable via the API, so the pneumonia-specificity null rests on the structured classification/510k metadata retrieved.

coverage_gaps_expected: openFDA device/denovo endpoint returns 404 - the founding De Novo (DEN-) grants that created QFM/QAS/QDQ/QBS could not be verified; none are cited

FDA 510(k) summary PDFs / decision summaries - openFDA returns structured metadata but not the free-text describing each cleared device's specific target findings or reported test statistics; confirming which QFM devices list pneumonia/consolidation as a target requires the per-K summary documents

FDA AI/ML-enabled device list & PCCP/GMLP guidance text - narrative FDA guidance not retrieved as primary sources; regulatory framing rests on peer-reviewed regulatory-science literature (PMID 37361549)
EU MDR / CE-mark records - no European regulatory database queried; the pathway above is US FDA only
Drugs@FDA / drug labeling / FAERS - not applicable (software/imaging device, not a drug or biologic); intentionally not used
Full-text of the cited methodology guidelines (CLAIM / STARD-AI / MRMC-framework) - only PubMed metadata + Crossref DOI resolution verified, not line-by-line content
PMID 39763559 (10.1101/2024.12.27.24319685) is a medRxiv PREPRINT - resolves at Crossref but is flagged not-peer-reviewed wherever cited
